sanskrit:
निश्चयं शृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम।
त्यागो हि पुरुषव्याघ्र त्रिविधः सम्प्रकीर्तितः॥
transliteration:
niścayaṃ śṛṇu me tatra tyāge bharatasattama |
tyāgo hi puruṣavyāghra trividhaḥ samprakīrtitaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Hear from Me the definite conclusion about renunciation, O best of the Bharatas; O tiger among men, renunciation is declared to be of three kinds.
contextual_meaning: Krishna signals that the topic of ‘letting go’ is nuanced and cannot be reduced to a single rule; he will now give a clinically precise taxonomy so Arjuna can locate his own style of relinquishing control without slipping into avoidance, perfectionism, or self-erasure.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative readiness—Arjuna is willing to examine his defenses around control and surrender, moving from global self-criticism to differentiated self-inquiry.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out caregiver told by friends to ‘just quit everything’ feels confused about what healthy boundaries actually look like; she scrolls for answers and lands on this verse promising three concrete types of ‘letting go’ rather than one impossible standard.
anchor_text: Let go in the way that keeps your heart engaged, not erased.
